title: Fix viewport click-vs-drag and camera control bugs
analysis:
Three high-severity viewport interaction bugs need fixing:

**V-10 (click-vs-drag):** In selection.ts line 97-100, `handlePointerDown` calls `raycast()` and `onSelect()` synchronously on every `pointerdown` event. This means every orbit-drag start fires an unwanted selection. Fix: track pointerdown position, add pointerup listener, only fire onSelect if pointer moved < 5px (standard click threshold). Existing tests in 'click-based selection raycasting' and 'rAF-throttled pointermove' sections dispatch `pointerdown` and expect immediate `onSelect` — these must be updated to dispatch pointerdown+pointerup.

**V-02 (Z-axis offset):** Both `fitToView` (line 175-176) and `flyToEntity` (line 199-200) do `camera.position.copy(center); camera.position.z += distance;` — this always offsets along world Z axis regardless of current view angle. Fix: use `camera.getWorldDirection()` to get current view direction, then position camera at `center - viewDir * distance`.

**V-03 (controls.target stale):** After fitToView/flyToEntity reposition the camera, `OrbitControls.target` is never updated. Subsequent orbit rotation pivots around the old target point, producing broken behavior. Fix: accept a controls reference in SelectionOptions, update `controls.target.copy(center)` in both fitToView and flyToEntity.

V-02 and V-03 are tightly coupled — both require changes to fitToView/flyToEntity and the SelectionOptions interface. The controls object (with its .target Vector3) needs to be threaded from Viewport.tsx into createSelection.

Existing test infrastructure (selection.test.ts) uses extensive Three.js mocks. The MockVector3 needs `sub()` and `multiplyScalar()` methods, and MockPerspectiveCamera needs `getWorldDirection()` to support the V-02 fix tests.

prerequisites.description: Extend mock classes in selection.test.ts: add `sub(v)`, `multiplyScalar(s)` to MockVector3; add `getWorldDirection(target)` to MockPerspectiveCamera. These are needed for V-02/V-03 tests but should be added upfront to avoid mixing mock infrastructure changes with test logic.

steps.description: V-10: Write failing tests for click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.test.ts. New describe block 'click-vs-drag discrimination' with tests: (1) pointerdown alone does NOT fire onSelect, (2) pointerdown+pointerup at same position fires onSelect with raycasted entity, (3) pointerdown+pointerup with >5px movement does NOT fire onSelect, (4) pointerdown+pointerup with <=5px movement fires onSelect, (5) onSelect receives null when pointerup raycast misses, (6) after dispose, pointerup does not fire onSelect.

V-10: Implement click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.ts. Replace `handlePointerDown` (which fires onSelect immediately) with: (a) `handlePointerDown` that records {clientX, clientY} and adds a one-shot pointerup listener, (b) `handlePointerUp` that computes Euclidean distance from pointerdown position and only calls raycast()+onSelect() if distance < 5px. Update dispose() to also remove pointerup listener. Update existing 'click-based selection raycasting' tests and 'rAF-throttled pointermove / pointerdown still raycasts synchronously' test to dispatch both pointerdown+pointerup events instead of just pointerdown.

V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for fitToView with view-direction offset and controls.target update. In selection.test.ts, add tests: (1) fitToView offsets camera along current view direction (not just Z) — set camera at an angle, verify position is center - viewDir*distance, (2) fitToView updates controls.target to bounding box center when controls provided via SelectionOptions, (3) fitToView still works without controls (backward compat). Update setup() to optionally accept a controls mock with a target Vector3.

V-02/V-03: Fix fitToView in selection.ts. Add optional `controls?: { target: { copy: (v: any) => void } }` field to SelectionOptions interface. In fitToView(): (a) get view direction via `camera.getWorldDirection(dir)`, (b) position camera at `center - viewDir * distance` instead of just Z offset, (c) if controls provided, set `controls.target.copy(center)`. Update existing fitToView tests that asserted Z-only offset to match new view-direction behavior.

V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for flyToEntity with view-direction offset and controls.target update. Tests: (1) flyToEntity offsets camera along current view direction, (2) flyToEntity updates controls.target to entity bounding box center, (3) flyToEntity with unknown entity does not crash or update controls.target.
V-02/V-03: Fix flyToEntity in selection.ts. Apply same view-direction offset and controls.target update pattern as fitToView. Update existing flyToEntity tests that asserted Z-only offset.

Viewport integration: Write failing test in Viewport.test.tsx verifying that createSelection is called with a controls option containing the OrbitControls instance from createControls. Assert that the mock createSelection receives `controls` in its options argument.

Viewport integration: Update Viewport.tsx to pass `controls: controls.controls` (the OrbitControls instance from ControlsContext) to createSelection's options. This threads the controls.target through so fitToView/flyToEntity can update it.

design_decisions.decision: Use duck-typed controls interface `{ target: { copy: (v: any) => void } }` in SelectionOptions rather than importing OrbitControls type directly
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps selection.ts decoupled from the specific controls implementation. Any controls object with a target Vector3 will work, improving testability and flexibility.

design_decisions.decision: 5px Euclidean distance threshold for click-vs-drag discrimination
design_decisions.rationale: Standard UX threshold used by most drag-detection libraries. Below 5px is within normal pointer jitter for intentional clicks. Matches the task description recommendation.

design_decisions.decision: Listen for pointerup on domElement (not document) for click detection
design_decisions.rationale: If the user starts a drag on the viewport and releases outside, we correctly treat it as a drag (no selection). This matches OrbitControls' own event scope and avoids global listener cleanup complexity.

design_decisions.decision: Record pointerdown position and use pointerup for selection instead of timing-based approach
design_decisions.rationale: Position-based discrimination is more robust than timing-based. A slow deliberate click should still select, and a fast small drag should not. Distance is the correct discriminator.

design_decisions.decision: Camera repositioning uses getWorldDirection to maintain viewing angle
design_decisions.rationale: When a user hits 'Fit to View', they expect the scene to fill the viewport while preserving their current viewing angle (e.g., looking from above). Offsetting only along Z always shows the front-on view, which is disorienting if the user was looking from a different angle.

design_decisions.decision: Make controls optional in SelectionOptions for backward compatibility
design_decisions.rationale: The selection system should work without controls (e.g., in tests or headless usage). When controls is not provided, fitToView/flyToEntity simply skip the target update.

reuse.what: Existing mock infrastructure in selection.test.ts (MockVector3, MockPerspectiveCamera, MockBox3, setup() helper, createMockDomElement, createMockMesh)
reuse.how: Extend existing mock classes with new methods (sub, multiplyScalar, getWorldDirection) rather than creating new mocks. Reuse setup() helper with minor additions for controls parameter.

reuse.what: Existing raycaster and NDC computation in selection.ts
reuse.how: The click-vs-drag fix reuses the existing raycast() function — it just delays the call from pointerdown to pointerup after threshold check.

reuse.what: Existing Viewport.test.tsx mock structure for createSelection
reuse.how: The mock for createSelection already captures its options argument. We can inspect the captured options to verify controls is passed.

reuse.what: ControlsContext.controls property already exposes OrbitControls instance
reuse.how: No changes needed to controls.ts — the OrbitControls instance with its .target property is already accessible via ControlsContext.controls.
